says: Respect the repository maintenance contract: keep bootstrap behavior generic, prefer .agents/repo.json for project-specific knobs, and sync AGENTS.md when commands, hooks, agent assets, or descriptor rules change.

Reusable .local caches, source mirrors, and bootstrap artifacts are accelerators. A miss must not be fatal in normal bootstrap or CI restore paths; only explicit upload/publish maintenance commands should fail on missing configured inputs.

If a command both builds and consumes build output, provide a --no-build or equivalent reuse mode. CI should run the explicit build once, then use reuse-mode test/package/bench/system-test steps to avoid duplicate rebuilds and make failures stage-specific.

Prefer underscores over hyphens for new files, directories, config keys, Python modules, generated identifiers, cache keys, and internal command names. CLI arguments still use conventional - / -- flags. Preserve existing hyphenated public interfaces unless deliberately migrating them with compatibility shims.
Keep .editorconfig as the template editor baseline. Add local VSCode plugins through .agents/repo.json:vscode_extensions and tools/setup rather than hard-coding project-specific extension paths.
